rulings.Case Title: SHARON WOODY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Co.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420 is denied. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to CRC 3.1342(a), which requires 45 days notice. The proof of service demonstrates that defendant served plaintiff by mail on February 2, 2012. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MOORES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al*****TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿S Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure § 397(C)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC¿s joinder in Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s motion for change of venue is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the underlying motion. (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. LEONARD et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 6, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff Sandra Leonard¿s loss of consortium cause of action is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that Mrs. Leonard¿s cause of action is barred and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this cause of action. Since all of Mr. Leonard¿s claimed exposure to asbestos pre-dated his marriage with Mrs. Leonard, Mrs. Leonard does not have a viable claim for loss of consortium. (Zwicker v. Altamont Emergency Room Physicians Medical Group (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 26.) Plaintiffs¿ argument that Zwicker is inapplicable is belied by the language of Zwicker. (See also Owens-Illinois, Inc. v. Cook (MD 2005) 872 A.2d 969, 982-84 (an asbestos exposure case which refers to Zwicker as among those cases that hold that ¿the marriage requirement for a loss of consortium claim is absolute, [and] that if the marriage post-dated the injury, there is no consortium claim.¿)). The doctrines of law of the case and collateral estoppel are inapplicable. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. LEONARD et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 6, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages and granted as to plaintiff Sandra Leonard¿s loss of consortium cause of action. As to punitive damages, defendants sustained their burden of production. Plaintiffs submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendants acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendants¿ objections to Mr. Crum¿s deposition testimony are overruled. Defendants¿ objections to Mr. Crosby¿s deposition testimony are overruled. As to loss of consortium, defendants satisfied their burden of production of making a prima facie case that Mrs. Leonard¿s cause of action is barred and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this cause of action. Since all of Mr. Leonard¿s claimed exposure to asbestos pre-dated his marriage with Mrs. Leonard, Mrs. Leonard does not have a viable claim for loss of consortium. (Zwicker v. Altamont Emergency Room Physicians Medical Group (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 26.) Plaintiffs¿ argument that Zwicker is inapplicable is belied by the language of Zwicker. (See also Owens-Illinois, Inc. v. Cook (MD 2005) 872 A.2d 969, 982-84 (an asbestos exposure case which refers to Zwicker as among those cases that hold that ¿the marriage requirement for a loss of consortium claim is absolute, [and] that if the marriage post-dated the injury, there is no consortium claim.¿)). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 6, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant John K. Bice Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to July 18. Plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is granted. Plaintiffs demonstrated that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot, for reasons stated, be presented. Supplemental opposition, if any, due by July 3. Supplemental reply, if any, due by July 13. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)
